97	
98	## Scoring
99	
100	The unified scoring system stores competition results in `scoresTable`, replacing a legacy results + sets approach.
101	
102	Key design:
103	- Scores encode values as integers for efficient DB-level sorting
104	- `competitionEventId` links to the competition event (NULL for personal logs)
105	- `scheme` and `scoreType` determine how the raw value is interpreted
106	- Tiebreak values stored separately for time-capped workouts
107	- Score rounds (`scoreRoundsTable`) store per-round breakdowns for multi-round workouts
108	
109	### Time input parsing matches save semantics
110	
111	Bare numeric input on a time score (no colon — e.g. `2000`) is treated as **raw seconds** in both the live preview and the save path, so the two can never disagree.
112	
113	[[apps/wodsmith-start/src/lib/scoring/parse/time.ts#parseTime]] defers to [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/lib/scoring/encode/time.ts#encodeTime]] for any non-colon input. An earlier version smart-padded bare digits as MM:SS (`2000` → `20:00` in preview) while the save persisted `33:20`, which confused athletes typing on a phone. Colon-delimited input (`12:34`, `1:02:34.567`) is unchanged. Use `precision: "ms"` only when explicitly persisting raw milliseconds.
114	
115	### Missing scores tie at worst place
116	
117	Athletes registered in a division who never submitted a score for an event are awarded the same points as if they finished one place behind every recorded score in their division.
118	
119	If a division has 5 recorded scores for an event, every athlete in that division without a score ties at place 6 and receives the algorithm-appropriate points for place 6 (e.g. 6 points under online scoring, `firstPlacePoints − 5×step` under traditional). [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-leaderboard.ts#getCompetitionLeaderboard]] computes `missingPlace = activeCount + 1` per (event, division) pair and looks up the points via [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/lib/scoring/algorithms/index.ts#calculatePointsForPlace]], then pushes an `eventResult` with `formattedScore: "N/A"` and adds the points to `totalPoints`. Without this, missing-score totals would default to 0 and outrank scored athletes under "lowest total wins" algorithms like `online`. Divisions where no athlete has scored an event still fall through to the empty-results fallback (rank 0, 0 points, not added to totals).

file.content:
Stored in the `movements` table with tags for filtering and search.

## Scoring

The unified scoring system stores competition results in `scoresTable`, replacing a legacy results + sets approach.

Key design:
- Scores encode values as integers for efficient DB-level sorting
- `competitionEventId` links to the competition event (NULL for personal logs)
- `scheme` and `scoreType` determine how the raw value is interpreted
- Tiebreak values stored separately for time-capped workouts
- Score rounds (`scoreRoundsTable`) store per-round breakdowns for multi-round workouts

### Time input parsing matches save semantics

Bare numeric input on a time score (no colon — e.g. `2000`) is treated as **raw seconds** in both the live preview and the save path, so the two can never disagree.

[[apps/wodsmith-start/src/lib/scoring/parse/time.ts#parseTime]] defers to [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/lib/scoring/encode/time.ts#encodeTime]] for any non-colon input. An earlier version smart-padded bare digits as MM:SS (`2000` → `20:00` in preview) while the save persisted `33:20`, which confused athletes typing on a phone. Colon-delimited input (`12:34`, `1:02:34.567`) is unchanged. Use `precision: "ms"` only when explicitly persisting raw milliseconds.

### Missing scores tie at worst place

Athletes registered in a division who never submitted a score for an event are awarded the same points as if they finished one place behind every recorded score in their division.

If a division has 5 recorded scores for an event, every athlete in that division without a score ties at place 6 and receives the algorithm-appropriate points for place 6 (e.g. 6 points under online scoring, `firstPlacePoints − 5×step` under traditional). [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-leaderboard.ts#getCompetitionLeaderboard]] computes `missingPlace = activeCount + 1` per (event, division) pair and looks up the points via [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/lib/scoring/algorithms/index.ts#calculatePointsForPlace]], then pushes an `eventResult` with `formattedScore: "N/A"` and adds the points to `totalPoints`. Without this, missing-score totals would default to 0 and outrank scored athletes under "lowest total wins" algorithms like `online`. Divisions where no athlete has scored an event still fall through to the empty-results fallback (rank 0, 0 points, not added to totals).

input.old_string:
Key design:
- Scores encode values as integers for efficient DB-level sorting
- `competitionEventId` links to the competition event (NULL for personal logs)

input.new_string:
Key design:
- Scores encode values as integers for efficient DB-level sorting
- `competitionEventId` links to the competition event (NULL for personal logs)
- `scheme` and `scoreType` determine how the raw value is interpreted
- Tiebreak values stored separately for time-capped workouts
- Score rounds (`scoreRoundsTable`) store per-round breakdowns for multi-round workouts

### One score per athlete per event per division

The `scoresTable` unique index spans `(competition_event_id, user_id, scaling_level_id)`, not just `(event, user)`. Online competitions allow the same workout to appear in multiple divisions (e.g. the same track workout scheduled for both an Individual and a Partner division), and athletes can register in more than one division. Keying only on `(event, user)` would merge an athlete's individual and partner scores into one row via `onDuplicateKeyUpdate` — the last submitted score would overwrite the other, and every leaderboard/athlete-detail read (which joins on `event + user`) would surface that single row against every registration the user owned, leaking partner scores onto the individual leaderboard and vice versa.

With `scaling_level_id` in the key, an athlete holds a separate row per division. Every score-write path ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/video-submission-fns.ts#submitVideoFn]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/competition-score-fns.ts#saveCompetitionScoreFn]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/athlete-score-fns.ts#submitAthleteScoreFn]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/submission-verification-fns.ts]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/api/compete/scores/submit.ts]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/api/compete/scores/judge.ts]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/api/compete/video/submit.ts]]) scopes the registration lookup by divisionId and carries that divisionId through to the upsert's `scalingLevelId`. Submission fns that accept an athlete without a divisionId reject ambiguously when the athlete is registered in more than one division rather than silently picking one. Every score-read path ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-leaderboard.ts#getCompetitionLeaderboard]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/series-leaderboard.ts]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/organizer-athlete-fns.ts#getOrganizerAthleteDetailFn]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/division-results-fns.ts]]) scopes scores by `scaling_level_id` and groups/attributes by the score's own `scalingLevelId` rather than by the first registration found for that userId — the leaderboard maps each score to the exact `(user, division)` registration, not just userId. Delete paths ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/competition-score-fns.ts#deleteCompetitionScoreFn]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/registration-fns.ts#removeRegistrationFn]]) scope by division so removing one registration or score doesn't nuke the athlete's scores in their other division on a shared workout. The registration-transfer path in [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/commerce/transfer-handlers.ts]] intentionally deletes all of the source user's scores across divisions because the user is being fully transferred out of the competition.

toolUseResult.oldString:
Key design:
- Scores encode values as integers for efficient DB-level sorting
- `competitionEventId` links to the competition event (NULL for personal logs)
- `scheme` and `scoreType` determine how the raw value is interpreted
- Tiebreak values stored separately for time-capped workouts
- Score rounds (`scoreRoundsTable`) store per-round breakdowns for multi-round workouts
toolUseResult.newString:
Key design:
- Scores encode values as integers for efficient DB-level sorting
- `competitionEventId` links to the competition event (NULL for personal logs)
- `scheme` and `scoreType` determine how the raw value is interpreted
- Tiebreak values stored separately for time-capped workouts
- Score rounds (`scoreRoundsTable`) store per-round breakdowns for multi-round workouts

### One score per athlete per event per division

The `scoresTable` unique index spans `(competition_event_id, user_id, scaling_level_id)`, not just `(event, user)`. Online competitions allow the same workout to appear in multiple divisions (e.g. the same track workout scheduled for both an Individual and a Partner division), and athletes can register in more than one division. Keying only on `(event, user)` would merge an athlete's individual and partner scores into one row via `onDuplicateKeyUpdate` — the last submitted score would overwrite the other, and every leaderboard/athlete-detail read (which joins on `event + user`) would surface that single row against every registration the user owned, leaking partner scores onto the individual leaderboard and vice versa.

With `scaling_level_id` in the key, an athlete holds a separate row per division. Every score-write path ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/video-submission-fns.ts#submitVideoFn]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/competition-score-fns.ts#saveCompetitionScoreFn]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/athlete-score-fns.ts#submitAthleteScoreFn]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/submission-verification-fns.ts]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/api/compete/scores/submit.ts]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/api/compete/scores/judge.ts]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/api/compete/video/submit.ts]]) scopes the registration lookup by divisionId and carries that divisionId through to the upsert's `scalingLevelId`. Submission fns that accept an athlete without a divisionId reject ambiguously when the athlete is registered in more than one division rather than silently picking one. Every score-read path ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-leaderboard.ts#getCompetitionLeaderboard]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/series-leaderboard.ts]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/organizer-athlete-fns.ts#getOrganizerAthleteDetailFn]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/division-results-fns.ts]]) scopes scores by `scaling_level_id` and groups/attributes by the score's own `scalingLevelId` rather than by the first registration found for that userId — the leaderboard maps each score to the exact `(user, division)` registration, not just userId. Delete paths ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/competition-score-fns.ts#deleteCompetitionScoreFn]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/registration-fns.ts#removeRegistrationFn]]) scope by division so removing one registration or score doesn't nuke the athlete's scores in their other division on a shared workout. The registration-transfer path in [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/commerce/transfer-handlers.ts]] intentionally deletes all of the source user's scores across divisions because the user is being fully transferred out of the competition.

- Score rounds (`scoreRoundsTable`) store per-round breakdowns for multi-round workouts
+### One score per athlete per event per division

+The `scoresTable` unique index spans `(competition_event_id, user_id, scaling_level_id)`, not just `(event, user)`. Online competitions allow the same workout to appear in multiple divisions (e.g. the same track workout scheduled for both an Individual and a Partner division), and athletes can register in more than one division. Keying only on `(event, user)` would merge an athlete's individual and partner scores into one row via `onDuplicateKeyUpdate` — the last submitted score would overwrite the other, and every leaderboard/athlete-detail read (which joins on `event + user`) would surface that single row against every registration the user owned, leaking partner scores onto the individual leaderboard and vice versa.

+With `scaling_level_id` in the key, an athlete holds a separate row per division. Every score-write path ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/video-submission-fns.ts#submitVideoFn]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/competition-score-fns.ts#saveCompetitionScoreFn]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/athlete-score-fns.ts#submitAthleteScoreFn]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/submission-verification-fns.ts]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/api/compete/scores/submit.ts]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/api/compete/scores/judge.ts]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/api/compete/video/submit.ts]]) scopes the registration lookup by divisionId and carries that divisionId through to the upsert's `scalingLevelId`. Submission fns that accept an athlete without a divisionId reject ambiguously when the athlete is registered in more than one division rather than silently picking one. Every score-read path ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-leaderboard.ts#getCompetitionLeaderboard]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/series-leaderboard.ts]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/organizer-athlete-fns.ts#getOrganizerAthleteDetailFn]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/division-results-fns.ts]]) scopes scores by `scaling_level_id` and groups/attributes by the score's own `scalingLevelId` rather than by the first registration found for that userId — the leaderboard maps each score to the exact `(user, division)` registration, not just userId. Delete paths ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/competition-score-fns.ts#deleteCompetitionScoreFn]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/registration-fns.ts#removeRegistrationFn]]) scope by division so removing one registration or score doesn't nuke the athlete's scores in their other division on a shared workout. The registration-transfer path in [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/commerce/transfer-handlers.ts]] intentionally deletes all of the source user's scores across divisions because the user is being fully transferred out of the competition.
### Time input parsing matches save semantics
Bare numeric input on a time score (no colon — e.g. `2000`) is treated as **raw seconds** in both the live preview and the save path, so the two can never disagree.

MySQL treats NULL as distinct in unique indexes, so personal logs (competitionEventId = NULL) are unaffected.

content.description:
Make every path that writes to scoresTable identify the correct registration by (eventId, userId, divisionId) and carry that divisionId through to the upsert's scalingLevelId. Paths:
- apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/athlete-score-fns.ts:319 (submitAthleteScoreFn) — add divisionId to input schema, filter registration lookup, remove .limit(1) ambiguity.
- apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/video-submission-fns.ts:1113 (submitVideoFn upsert) — already scopes registration, confirm upsert works correctly with new 3-col unique key.
- apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/api/compete/scores/submit.ts:290 — already accepts divisionId; confirm upsert works.
- apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/api/compete/video/submit.ts:301 — check divisionId scoping.
- apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/api/compete/scores/judge.ts:296 — check scalingLevelId.

content.subject: Fix read paths to scope scores by scalingLevelId
content.description:
Scores joined back by (competitionEventId, userId) currently leak across divisions. Fix:
- apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-leaderboard.ts:740-744 (fetchScores) + :878-894 grouping — scope to the registration's divisionId, and bucket by score.scalingLevelId (the ground truth), not registration.registration.divisionId.
- apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/organizer-athlete-fns.ts:366-374 — add scalingLevelId filter matching the registration's division.
- apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/athlete-score-fns.ts getAthleteEventScoreFn (~line 260-314) — add divisionId filter so the submission panel loads the right existing score.
- any other score-read helpers that key only on (competitionEventId, userId).

content.text:
Done. Ready for you to confirm.

**SQL to run**: `apps/wodsmith-start/sql/score-division-unique.sql`

```sql
ALTER TABLE `scores` DROP INDEX `idx_scores_competition_user_unique`;
ALTER TABLE `scores`
  ADD CONSTRAINT `idx_scores_competition_user_unique`
  UNIQUE (`competition_event_id`, `user_id`, `scaling_level_id`);
```

Safe — the old 2-col unique guarantees at most one row per `(event, user)`, so widening to 3 cols can't create collisions.

**Code changes**:
- Schema: `scores.ts` unique index now spans `(event, user, scalingLevel)`.
- Writes scope registration lookups by `divisionId` and reject ambiguity when a user is in multiple divisions: `athlete-score-fns.ts`, `scores/submit.ts`, `video/submit.ts`. All upserts and post-upsert score lookups (`scores/judge.ts`, `competition-score-fns.ts`, `submission-verification-fns.ts`, `video-submission-fns.ts`) now key by `scalingLevelId`.
- Reads now bucket by `score.scalingLevelId` (ground truth) and match each score to its `(user, division)` registration: `competition-leaderboard.ts`, `series-leaderboard.ts`, `organizer-athlete-fns.ts`, `division-results-fns.ts`, `competition-score-fns.ts`.
- Delete paths scoped by division so removing one registration doesn't nuke scores in other divisions: `competition-score-fns.ts#deleteCompetitionScoreFn`, `registration-fns.ts#removeRegistrationFn`. Transfer handler intentionally left wide (user leaves the comp entirely).
- Client: `event-score-card.tsx` passes `divisionId` when deleting.
- `lat.md/domain.md` updated with the new invariant; `lat check` passes.

`pnpm type-check` clean. I couldn't run `db:push` (credentials not available in this env), so you'll need to apply the SQL manually or via your normal path, then confirm the behavior on the preview leaderboard + `/athletes/creg_…` page.

[[lat.md/registration#Registration#Multi-Division Registration]] (lat.md/registration.md:48-53)

> ## Multi-Division Registration
>
> Athletes can register for multiple divisions in a single checkout session.
>
> Each division becomes a separate line item in Stripe Checkout and a separate `commercePurchaseTable` record. Free divisions within a mixed checkout are registered immediately while paid ones go through Stripe. The `items` array in the input schema supports this, with duplicate division validation.
>

* `lat section "section#id"` — show full content with outgoing/incoming refs
* `lat search "new query"` — search for something else
---
[[lat.md/organizer-dashboard#Organizer Dashboard#Leaderboard Preview]] (lat.md/organizer-dashboard.md:121-126)

> ## Leaderboard Preview
>
> Organizer-only leaderboard that shows aggregated standings including unpublished division results and draft events, letting admins review the full picture before they hit publish.
>
> Lives in the "Run Competition" sidebar group at `/compete/organizer/{competitionId}/leaderboard-preview`. The route ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/compete/organizer/$competitionId/leaderboard-preview.tsx]]) reuses the public [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/components/leaderboard-page-content.tsx]] component with a `preview` prop. When `preview` is set, [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/leaderboard-fns.ts#getCompetitionLeaderboardFn]] runs a server-side `requireTeamPermission(MANAGE_COMPETITIONS)` check against the organizing team and then calls [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-leaderboard.ts#getCompetitionLeaderboard]] with `bypassPublicationFilter: true`. That flag skips both gates the public leaderboard enforces: (1) the `eventStatus = 'published'` filter on track workouts, so scores entered on draft events still appear; and (2) the per-division `divisionResults` publish check, so every scored division is included regardless of publish state. Athletes never hit this route; the public leaderboard at `/compete/{slug}/leaderboard` always passes `preview = false` and goes through the standard publish gating.
>

content.content:
[[lat.md/domain#Domain Model#Video Submissions#Multi-Division Submission]] (lat.md/domain.md:181-186)

> ### Multi-Division Submission
>
> Athletes registered in multiple divisions see a division picker on the submission form. Single-division athletes see a static badge.
>
> Switching divisions fetches that division's submission data via [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/video-submission-fns.ts#getVideoSubmissionFn]] with the `divisionId` parameter. Scores and video submissions are scoped per-division so each registration gets its own submission state. The picker lives in [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/components/compete/video-submission-form.tsx#VideoSubmissionForm]]. When event-division mappings are configured, the division picker is filtered to only show divisions mapped to the current event — the route computes `filteredRegisteredDivisions` using the same mapping logic as the division tabs. The route loader uses `loaderDeps` to pass the URL `?division=` search param into the loader, so the initial submission data is fetched for the URL-selected division rather than always defaulting to the first registered division. The loader also constrains the initial division to the event-mapped set (not just athlete-registered divisions), preventing fetches for unmapped divisions that would produce mismatched form state. This ensures team divisions (teamSize > 1) initialize the form with the correct number of video slots. The form accepts an `initialDivisionId` prop and syncs its internal state directly from the loader-provided `initialData` when the URL division changes — no redundant fetch is needed because the loader already re-runs via `loaderDeps`.
>

file.content:
**Important**: This function does NOT send notifications. Callers are responsible for sending confirmation emails with the appropriate payment context.

## Multi-Division Registration

Athletes can register for multiple divisions in a single checkout session.

Each division becomes a separate line item in Stripe Checkout and a separate `commercePurchaseTable` record. Free divisions within a mixed checkout are registered immediately while paid ones go through Stripe. The `items` array in the input schema supports this, with duplicate division validation.

## Team Registration

Team divisions (teamSize > 1) require a team name and teammate emails during registration.

The captain (registering user) creates the team. A `competition_team` is created in the `teamTable` with `competitionMetadata` JSON storing `competitionId` and `divisionId`. Teammates are invited via [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/registration.ts#inviteUserToTeamInternal]]: existing users are added directly to the team; new users receive an email invitation with a 30-day token. Teammates are also added to the `competition_event` team. Captains can refresh expired invites via [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/registration-fns.ts#refreshCompetitionTeamInviteFn]], which generates a new token, extends the expiry by 30 days, and resends the email.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Registration

The registration process connects athletes to competitions, handling payment, capacity checks, team formation, and notifications.

## Registration Flow

Two entry points converge on the same core logic: athlete self-registration and organizer manual registration.

### Athlete Self-Registration

Athletes register themselves via `initiateRegistrationPaymentFn`, which handles both free and paid competitions.

The flow validates in order:
1. Registration window is open (checked against competition timezone)
2. Not already registered for the selected division(s)
3. Division capacity not exceeded
4. Competition-wide capacity not exceeded
5. Required registration questions answered
6. Fee calculated per division (division-specific fee overrides competition default)
7. Coupon applied if provided

For **free** competitions (or fully discounted by coupon): calls `registerForCompetition` directly and returns immediately.

For **paid** competitions: creates `commercePurchaseTable` records (one per division), builds Stripe Checkout line items with fee breakdown, creates a Stripe Checkout Session, and redirects the athlete. Registration is finalized asynchronously by the [[registration#Stripe Checkout Workflow]].

### Organizer Manual Registration

Organizers register athletes from the dashboard via `createManualRegistrationFn`, bypassing the registration window.

If the athlete email doesn't exist, a placeholder user is created with a personal team. The organizer sets payment status to `COMP` (complimentary) or `PAID_OFFLINE`. A claim token is generated for new users so they can create an account and claim their registration. Confirmation email is sent via the [[registration#Manual Registration Workflow]].

## Core Registration Logic

The `registerForCompetition` function in `src/server/registration.ts` is the shared core for both entry points.

It handles:
- Competition and division validation (division must belong to competition's scaling group)
- Duplicate registration prevention (per division, not per competition — multi-division is allowed)
- Team division logic: creates a `competition_team`, adds captain, stores pending teammates as JSON
- Team name uniqueness check (case-insensitive within competition)
- Teammate conflict checks: not already registered, not on another team, not already invited in same division
- Adds athlete to the `competition_event` team as a member
- Upserts affiliate name to the affiliates table
- Updates user sessions to include new team memberships

**Important**: This function does NOT send notifications. Callers are responsible for sending confirmation emails with the appropriate payment context.

## Multi-Division Registration

Athletes can register for multiple divisions in a single checkout session.

Each division becomes a separate line item in Stripe Checkout and a separate `commercePurchaseTable` record. Free divisions within a mixed checkout are registered immediately while paid ones go through Stripe. The `items` array in the input schema supports this, with duplicate division validation.

## Team Registration

Team divisions (teamSize > 1) require a team name and teammate emails during registration.

The captain (registering user) creates the team. A `competition_team` is created in the `teamTable` with `competitionMetadata` JSON storing `competitionId` and `divisionId`. Teammates are invited via [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/registration.ts#inviteUserToTeamInternal]]: existing users are added directly to the team; new users receive an email invitation with a 30-day token. Teammates are also added to the `competition_event` team. Captains can refresh expired invites via [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/registration-fns.ts#refreshCompetitionTeamInviteFn]], which generates a new token, extends the expiry by 30 days, and resends the email.

Organizers manage roster composition from the [[organizer-dashboard#Registrations (Athletes)#Athlete Detail Page]] without captaincy: [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/organizer-athlete-fns.ts#addTeammateToRegistrationFn]] creates a pending invite directly (bypasses the captain-only `INVITE_MEMBERS` check by inlining the invite insert + email send), [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/organizer-athlete-fns.ts#removeTeammateFromRegistrationFn]] deactivates a single athlete-team membership (and the matching event-team membership) without touching the captain, [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/organizer-athlete-fns.ts#cancelPendingTeamInviteFn]] sets a pending invite to `CANCELLED`, and [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/organizer-athlete-fns.ts#resendTeamInviteAsOrganizerFn]] resends any pending invite regardless of expiry (vs. the captain-only fn which only permits *expired* refreshes).

## Capacity Management

Both division-level and competition-wide capacity are enforced at registration time and again at payment completion.

Division capacity uses `calculateDivisionCapacity` with `divisionMaxSpots` (per-division override) falling back to `competitionDefaultMax`. Competition-wide capacity checks `maxTotalRegistrations`. Pending purchases (within a time window) count toward occupied spots to prevent overselling during concurrent checkouts.

## Stripe Checkout Workflow

A Cloudflare Workflow that processes `checkout.session.completed` events from Stripe webhooks.

Three durable steps with independent retries:
1. **create-registration**: Idempotency checks (by purchaseId and by user+division), re-checks capacity (refunds automatically if full), creates registration, stores answers, records payment in financial events
2. **send-confirmation-email**: Sends registration confirmation email (non-blocking — failure doesn't block step 3)
3. **send-slack-notification**: Sends Slack notification for team visibility

If division or competition fills during payment, the workflow automatically issues a Stripe refund and marks the purchase as `FAILED`. In local dev, `processCheckoutInline` runs the same logic synchronously.

## Manual Registration Workflow

A Cloudflare Workflow that sends confirmation emails for organizer-created registrations.

Counts pending waivers for the athlete, then sends a confirmation email. For placeholder users, the email includes a claim URL. Single step with 3 retries and exponential backoff. In local dev, `processManualRegistrationInline` runs synchronously.

## Registration Removal

Organizers can soft-delete registrations via `removeRegistrationFn`, setting status to `REMOVED`.

Cascading cleanup: deactivates team memberships (captain in event team + all members in athlete team), cancels pending invitations, deletes heat assignments, and deletes scores for the registered user(s) across all competition events. Requires `MANAGE_COMPETITIONS` permission.

## Division Transfer

Organizers can move a registration between divisions via `transferRegistrationDivisionFn`.

Validates same team size between source and target divisions (individual-to-team blocked). Updates the registration's `divisionId`, removes heat assignments (division-specific), and updates the commerce purchase record. Does not enforce capacity (organizer decision).

lines: Each division becomes a separate line item in Stripe Checkout and a separate `commercePurchaseTable` record. Free divisions within a mixed checkout are registered immediately while paid ones go through Stripe. The `items` array in the input schema supports this, with duplicate division validation.

+Downstream, scores stay scoped per division — see [[lat.md/domain#Domain Model#Scoring#One score per athlete per event per division]] for the unique key and write/read contracts that prevent a partner-division score from leaking onto the individual leaderboard when the same track workout is shared across both divisions.
Team divisions (teamSize > 1) require a team name and teammate emails during registration.

input.subject: Note track_workout_id semantics in SQL + docs
input.description: scores.competition_event_id actually stores a track_workout_id (PK of track_workouts), not competition_events.id. Every insert uses data.trackWorkoutId. The migration SQL is unchanged (column name is the same, functional behavior is identical — the scope of "one score per track_workout per user per division" is exactly what we want since a shared workout across divisions has ONE track_workout row, gated per division via event-division mappings). Update the SQL file comment to flag the misleading column name and update the lat.md Scoring invariant to use track_workout wording so future readers aren't misled.

input.old_string:
-- Widen the scoresTable unique index so one athlete can hold a distinct score
-- per division on a shared workout. Before: unique(competition_event_id, user_id)
-- collapsed an athlete's individual + partner scores into one row via
-- onDuplicateKeyUpdate, and every leaderboard/athlete-detail read keyed by
-- (event, user) pulled that single row onto every registration the user owned.
--
-- Safe to run: the existing 2-column unique guarantees at most one row per
-- (event, user) pair, so widening to 3 columns cannot produce duplicates.
input.new_string:
-- Widen the scoresTable unique index so one athlete can hold a distinct score
-- per division on a shared workout. Before: unique(competition_event_id, user_id)
-- collapsed an athlete's individual + partner scores into one row via
-- onDuplicateKeyUpdate, and every leaderboard/athlete-detail read keyed by
-- (event, user) pulled that single row onto every registration the user owned.
--
-- Column-name note: `scores.competition_event_id` is misleadingly named — it
-- actually stores a `track_workouts.id`, not a `competition_events.id`. Every
-- insert assigns it from the caller's `trackWorkoutId`. That's exactly the
-- right scope here: when the same workout appears in multiple divisions via
-- event-division mappings, there is one track_workout row referenced by both
-- divisions, so the unique key `(track_workout_id, user_id, scaling_level_id)`
-- is what lets a multi-division athlete hold one score per division.
--
-- Safe to run: the existing 2-column unique guarantees at most one row per
-- (track_workout, user) pair, so widening to 3 columns cannot produce
-- duplicates on existing data.

input.old_string:
### One score per athlete per event per division

The `scoresTable` unique index spans `(competition_event_id, user_id, scaling_level_id)` so an athlete registered in multiple divisions can hold a separate score per division on a shared workout.

Online competitions allow the same track workout to appear in multiple divisions (e.g. scheduled for both Individual and Partner), and athletes can register in more than one division. Keying only on `(event, user)` would merge an athlete's individual and partner scores into one row via `onDuplicateKeyUpdate` — the last submitted score would overwrite the other, and every leaderboard/athlete-detail read (which joins on `event + user`) would surface that single row against every registration the user owned, leaking partner scores onto the individual leaderboard and vice versa.

With `scaling_level_id` in the key, an athlete holds a separate row per division. Every score-write path ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/video-submission-fns.ts#submitVideoFn]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/competition-score-fns.ts#saveCompetitionScoreFn]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/athlete-score-fns.ts#submitAthleteScoreFn]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/submission-verification-fns.ts]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/api/compete/scores/submit.ts]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/api/compete/scores/judge.ts]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/api/compete/video/submit.ts]]) scopes the registration lookup by divisionId and carries that divisionId through to the upsert's `scalingLevelId`. Submission fns that accept an athlete without a divisionId reject ambiguously when the athlete is registered in more than one division rather than silently picking one. Every score-read path ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-leaderboard.ts#getCompetitionLeaderboard]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/series-leaderboard.ts]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/organizer-athlete-fns.ts#getOrganizerAthleteDetailFn]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/division-results-fns.ts]]) scopes scores by `scaling_level_id` and groups/attributes by the score's own `scalingLevelId` rather than by the first registration found for that userId — the leaderboard maps each score to the exact `(user, division)` registration, not just userId. Delete paths ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/competition-score-fns.ts#deleteCompetitionScoreFn]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/registration-fns.ts#removeRegistrationFn]]) scope by division so removing one registration or score doesn't nuke the athlete's scores in their other division on a shared workout. The registration-transfer path in [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/commerce/transfer-handlers.ts]] intentionally deletes all of the source user's scores across divisions because the user is being fully transferred out of the competition.
input.new_string:
### One score per athlete per event per division

The `scoresTable` unique index spans `(competition_event_id, user_id, scaling_level_id)` so an athlete registered in multiple divisions can hold a separate score per division on a shared workout.

Column-name note: `scores.competition_event_id` is misleadingly named — it actually stores a `track_workouts.id` (the id the caller supplies as `trackWorkoutId`), not a `competition_events.id`. Every insert path assigns it from `data.trackWorkoutId`. That scope is exactly right for this invariant: when the same workout appears in multiple divisions via event-division mappings, both divisions reference the same track_workout row, so the unique key `(track_workout, user, scaling_level)` is what lets a multi-division athlete carry one score per division.

Online competitions allow the same track workout to appear in multiple divisions (e.g. scheduled for both Individual and Partner), and athletes can register in more than one division. Keying only on `(track_workout, user)` would merge an athlete's individual and partner scores into one row via `onDuplicateKeyUpdate` — the last submitted score would overwrite the other, and every leaderboard/athlete-detail read (which joins on `track_workout + user`) would surface that single row against every registration the user owned, leaking partner scores onto the individual leaderboard and vice versa.

With `scaling_level_id` in the key, an athlete holds a separate row per division. Every score-write path ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/video-submission-fns.ts#submitVideoFn]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/competition-score-fns.ts#saveCompetitionScoreFn]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/athlete-score-fns.ts#submitAthleteScoreFn]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/submission-verification-fns.ts]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/api/compete/scores/submit.ts]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/api/compete/scores/judge.ts]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/api/compete/video/submit.ts]]) scopes the registration lookup by divisionId and carries that divisionId through to the upsert's `scalingLevelId`. Submission fns that accept an athlete without a divisionId reject ambiguously when the athlete is registered in more than one division rather than silently picking one. Every score-read path ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-leaderboard.ts#getCompetitionLeaderboard]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/series-leaderboard.ts]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/organizer-athlete-fns.ts#getOrganizerAthleteDetailFn]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/division-results-fns.ts]]) scopes scores by `scaling_level_id` and groups/attributes by the score's own `scalingLevelId` rather than by the first registration found for that userId — the leaderboard maps each score to the exact `(user, division)` registration, not just userId. Delete paths ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/competition-score-fns.ts#deleteCompetitionScoreFn]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/registration-fns.ts#removeRegistrationFn]]) scope by division so removing one registration or score doesn't nuke the athlete's scores in their other division on a shared workout. The registration-transfer path in [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/commerce/transfer-handlers.ts]] intentionally deletes all of the source user's scores across divisions because the user is being fully transferred out of the competition.

### One score per athlete per event per division

The `scoresTable` unique index spans `(competition_event_id, user_id, scaling_level_id)` so an athlete registered in multiple divisions can hold a separate score per division on a shared workout.

Online competitions allow the same track workout to appear in multiple divisions (e.g. scheduled for both Individual and Partner), and athletes can register in more than one division. Keying only on `(event, user)` would merge an athlete's individual and partner scores into one row via `onDuplicateKeyUpdate` — the last submitted score would overwrite the other, and every leaderboard/athlete-detail read (which joins on `event + user`) would surface that single row against every registration the user owned, leaking partner scores onto the individual leaderboard and vice versa. Every score-write path ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/video-submission-fns.ts#submitVideoFn]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/competition-score-fns.ts#saveCompetitionScoreFn]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/athlete-score-fns.ts#submitAthleteScoreFn]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/submission-verification-fns.ts]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/api/compete/scores/submit.ts]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/api/compete/scores/judge.ts]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/api/compete/video/submit.ts]]) scopes the registration lookup by divisionId and carries that divisionId through to the upsert's `scalingLevelId`. Submission fns that accept an athlete without a divisionId reject ambiguously when the athlete is registered in more than one division rather than silently picking one. Every score-read path ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-leaderboard.ts#getCompetitionLeaderboard]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/series-leaderboard.ts]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/organizer-athlete-fns.ts#getOrganizerAthleteDetailFn]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/division-results-fns.ts]]) scopes scores by `scaling_level_id` and groups/attributes by the score's own `scalingLevelId` rather than by the first registration found for that userId — the leaderboard maps each score to the exact `(user, division)` registration, not just userId. Delete paths ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/competition-score-fns.ts#deleteCompetitionScoreFn]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/registration-fns.ts#removeRegistrationFn]]) scope by division so removing one registration or score doesn't nuke the athlete's scores in their other division on a shared workout. The registration-transfer path in [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/commerce/transfer-handlers.ts]] intentionally deletes all of the source user's scores across divisions because the user is being fully transferred out of the competition.

### Time input parsing matches save semantics

Bare numeric input on a time score (no colon — e.g. `2000`) is treated as **raw seconds** in both the live preview and the save path, so the two can never disagree.

Delete paths ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/competition-score-fns.ts#deleteCompetitionScoreFn]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/registration-fns.ts#removeRegistrationFn]]) scope by division so removing one registration or score doesn't nuke the athlete's scores in their other division on a shared workout. The registration-transfer path in [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/commerce/transfer-handlers.ts]] intentionally deletes all of the source user's scores across divisions because the user is being fully transferred out of the competition.
### Time input parsing matches save semantics
Bare numeric input on a time score (no colon — e.g. `2000`) is treated as **raw seconds** in both the live preview and the save path, so the two can never disagree.

content.text:
Factored in. The DDL is unchanged — the column name is just misleading — but I added a prominent note to both the SQL file header and the lat.md invariant so the semantics are clear:

- `scores.competition_event_id` actually stores `track_workouts.id` (every insert writes `data.trackWorkoutId`).
- The scope is still correct: when the same workout appears in multiple divisions via event-division mappings, both divisions reference the **same** `track_workout` row, so widening the unique to `(track_workout_id, user_id, scaling_level_id)` is exactly what lets a multi-division athlete hold one score per division.

`lat check` passes.

input.description: Read results entry and leaderboard preview sections

content.content:
[[lat.md/organizer-dashboard#Organizer Dashboard#Results Entry]] (lat.md/organizer-dashboard.md:102-109)

> ## Results Entry
>
> Organizers enter scores for each athlete per event, or review video submissions for online competitions.
>
> For **in-person competitions**: `ResultsEntryForm` provides a per-event, per-division score entry grid. Organizers select an event and division, then enter scores for each athlete. Supports publishing/unpublishing division results. Multi-round scores flow through [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/competition-score-fns.ts#saveCompetitionScoreFn]], which for `time-with-cap` workouts derives per-round cap status server-side from each round's encoded value against `workout.timeCap * 1000`, persists that status on `scoreRoundsTable`, preserves the summed total on `scoresTable.scoreValue` (instead of clamping to the cap), and threads `cappedRoundCount` into `computeSortKey` so the leaderboard tiebreaker honors "fewer capped rounds wins".
>
> For **online competitions**: Shows a submissions overview with links to individual video verification pages at `/events/{eventId}/submissions/`. Layout mirrors the volunteer review index (`/compete/$slug/review`) — parent events render as cards with their child sub-events listed inline, each row showing per-event total / reviewed / pending counts and a progress bar. Counts come from [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/video-submission-fns.ts#getSubmissionCountsByEventFn]], which gates on `requireSubmissionReviewAccess(competitionId)`, filters the requested trackWorkoutIds to those belonging to the competition's programming track (so callers can't enumerate counts across tenants), then issues grouped `COUNT() ... GROUP BY trackWorkoutId` queries for total and reviewed counts — autochunked as needed to respect MySQL parameter limits. It inner-joins `competition_registrations` to exclude submissions from removed registrations, matching the leaderboard and in-person results entry filter. This replaces the prior per-event `getEventSubmissionsFn` fan-out, which materialized full submission rows (with autochunked user/division/registration lookups) just to compute three numbers per event. The per-event review list ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/video-submission-fns.ts#getOrganizerSubmissionsFn]]) applies the same removed-registration filter on its registration join.
>
> ### Division Results Publish Gate
>
> Controls whether scores for a given (event, division) pair are visible on the public leaderboard. Organizers toggle publish state per event-division from the results entry UI.
>
> Publish state lives in `competitionsTable.settings.divisionResults[trackWorkoutId][divisionId].publishedAt` — an ISO timestamp when published, absent/null when in draft. [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-leaderboard.ts#getCompetitionLeaderboard]] reads this shape and gates two flows with it:
>
> 1. Per-event leaderboard entries: inside the per-event loop, `if (!divisionPublishState?.publishedAt) continue` drops any (event, division) that isn't published.
> 2. Video visibility: the `isEventDivisionPublished` helper returns `false` for unpublished pairs so their videos don't leak.
>
> Defaults: when `divisionResults` is absent entirely, online competitions treat everything as hidden (opt-in publishing) while in-person competitions show everything (backwards compat for gyms that never opted into the gate). Organizers can bulk-publish all divisions for an event from `QuickActionsDivisionResults`.
---
[[lat.md/organizer-dashboard#Organizer Dashboard#Leaderboard Preview]] (lat.md/organizer-dashboard.md:121-126)

> ## Leaderboard Preview
>
> Organizer-only leaderboard that shows aggregated standings including unpublished division results and draft events, letting admins review the full picture before they hit publish.
>
> Lives in the "Run Competition" sidebar group at `/compete/organizer/{competitionId}/leaderboard-preview`. The route ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/compete/organizer/$competitionId/leaderboard-preview.tsx]]) reuses the public [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/components/leaderboard-page-content.tsx]] component with a `preview` prop. When `preview` is set, [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/leaderboard-fns.ts#getCompetitionLeaderboardFn]] runs a server-side `requireTeamPermission(MANAGE_COMPETITIONS)` check against the organizing team and then calls [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-leaderboard.ts#getCompetitionLeaderboard]] with `bypassPublicationFilter: true`. That flag skips both gates the public leaderboard enforces: (1) the `eventStatus = 'published'` filter on track workouts, so scores entered on draft events still appear; and (2) the per-division `divisionResults` publish check, so every scored division is included regardless of publish state. Athletes never hit this route; the public leaderboard at `/compete/{slug}/leaderboard` always passes `preview = false` and goes through the standard publish gating.
>

## This section references:

content.content:
[[lat.md/organizer-dashboard#Organizer Dashboard#Registrations (Athletes)]] (lat.md/organizer-dashboard.md:59-73)

> ## Registrations (Athletes)
>
> Lists all registered athletes with division, payment status, team info, and management actions.
>
> Uses `getOrganizerRegistrationsFn` for the full registration list with detailed athlete info. Features include:
> - Sortable table with division and status filters
> - Manual registration dialog (organizer adds athletes directly — see [[registration#Registration Flow#Organizer Manual Registration]])
> - Remove registration (soft-delete with cascading cleanup — see [[registration#Registration Removal]])
> - Transfer registration to a different division (see [[registration#Division Transfer]])
> - Transfer registration to a different person (purchase transfer)
> - CSV export of athlete data
> - Registration questions editor (custom questions athletes answer during signup)
> - Pending teammate invitations tab for team divisions
> - Per-athlete deep link to the [[organizer-dashboard#Registrations (Athletes)#Athlete Detail Page]] via the "View Details" item in the row's captain-scoped actions dropdown (the athlete name itself is plain text — not a link — to keep the row visually calm)
>
> ### Athlete Detail Page
>

The events list is filtered by event-division mappings using the registration's `divisionId`, mirroring the leaderboard rule (see [[organizer-dashboard#Event Management#Event-Division Mappings]]): when no mappings exist, every event is returned; when mappings exist, an event is included if neither it nor its parent has a mapping (unmapped → visible to every division) OR if it (or its parent, for sub-events) has a mapping for the registration's division. Scores and video submissions are scoped to the filtered event list, so the Scores / Video Submissions sections only show events the athlete is competing in.

The button is disabled when the registration has no `commercePurchaseId` (e.g. organizer-comped) or the registration is `removed`.

120	
121	## Leaderboard Preview
122	
123	Organizer-only leaderboard that shows aggregated standings including unpublished division results and draft events, letting admins review the full picture before they hit publish.
124	
125	Lives in the "Run Competition" sidebar group at `/compete/organizer/{competitionId}/leaderboard-preview`. The route ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/compete/organizer/$competitionId/leaderboard-preview.tsx]]) reuses the public [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/components/leaderboard-page-content.tsx]] component with a `preview` prop. When `preview` is set, [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/leaderboard-fns.ts#getCompetitionLeaderboardFn]] runs a server-side `requireTeamPermission(MANAGE_COMPETITIONS)` check against the organizing team and then calls [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-leaderboard.ts#getCompetitionLeaderboard]] with `bypassPublicationFilter: true`. That flag skips both gates the public leaderboard enforces: (1) the `eventStatus = 'published'` filter on track workouts, so scores entered on draft events still appear; and (2) the per-division `divisionResults` publish check, so every scored division is included regardless of publish state. Athletes never hit this route; the public leaderboard at `/compete/{slug}/leaderboard` always passes `preview = false` and goes through the standard publish gating.
126	
127	## Submission Windows
128	
129	Manages time windows during which athletes can submit video evidence for online competitions.
130	
131	Only available when `competitionType === "online"`. Fetches workouts and competition events. Each event can have a submission window with open/close dates. Uses `SubmissionWindowsManager` component. Only parent (top-level) events appear in the unassigned pool and are draggable; sub-events are automatically assigned to the same window as their parent and displayed as children on the window card.
132	
133	## Volunteers

file.content:
## Leaderboard Preview

Organizer-only leaderboard that shows aggregated standings including unpublished division results and draft events, letting admins review the full picture before they hit publish.

Lives in the "Run Competition" sidebar group at `/compete/organizer/{competitionId}/leaderboard-preview`. The route ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/compete/organizer/$competitionId/leaderboard-preview.tsx]]) reuses the public [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/components/leaderboard-page-content.tsx]] component with a `preview` prop. When `preview` is set, [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/leaderboard-fns.ts#getCompetitionLeaderboardFn]] runs a server-side `requireTeamPermission(MANAGE_COMPETITIONS)` check against the organizing team and then calls [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-leaderboard.ts#getCompetitionLeaderboard]] with `bypassPublicationFilter: true`. That flag skips both gates the public leaderboard enforces: (1) the `eventStatus = 'published'` filter on track workouts, so scores entered on draft events still appear; and (2) the per-division `divisionResults` publish check, so every scored division is included regardless of publish state. Athletes never hit this route; the public leaderboard at `/compete/{slug}/leaderboard` always passes `preview = false` and goes through the standard publish gating.

## Submission Windows

Manages time windows during which athletes can submit video evidence for online competitions.

Only available when `competitionType === "online"`. Fetches workouts and competition events. Each event can have a submission window with open/close dates. Uses `SubmissionWindowsManager` component. Only parent (top-level) events appear in the unassigned pool and are draggable; sub-events are automatically assigned to the same window as their parent and displayed as children on the window card.

## Volunteers

Lives in the "Run Competition" sidebar group at `/compete/organizer/{competitionId}/leaderboard-preview`. The route ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/compete/organizer/$competitionId/leaderboard-preview.tsx]]) reuses the public [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/components/leaderboard-page-content.tsx]] component with a `preview` prop. When `preview` is set, [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/leaderboard-fns.ts#getCompetitionLeaderboardFn]] runs a server-side `requireTeamPermission(MANAGE_COMPETITIONS)` check against the organizing team and then calls [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-leaderboard.ts#getCompetitionLeaderboard]] with `bypassPublicationFilter: true`. Athletes never hit this route; the public leaderboard at `/compete/{slug}/leaderboard` always passes `preview = false` and goes through the standard publish gating.

Preview and public both bucket scores by `score.scalingLevelId` (the score's own division), not by the first registration found for that userId, so a multi-division athlete's partner score is attributed to their partner registration and their individual score to their individual registration — even when both registrations share the same track workout. See [[lat.md/domain#Domain Model#Scoring#One score per athlete per event per division]] for the underlying unique-key contract.

content.content:
98	Configures the algorithm used to rank athletes on the leaderboard.
99	
100	Fetches events for head-to-head tiebreaker selection. Uses `ScoringSettingsForm` component. Settings include scoring algorithm, point distribution, and tiebreak rules.
101	
102	## Results Entry
103	
104	Organizers enter scores for each athlete per event, or review video submissions for online competitions.
105	
106	For **in-person competitions**: `ResultsEntryForm` provides a per-event, per-division score entry grid. Organizers select an event and division, then enter scores for each athlete. Supports publishing/unpublishing division results. Multi-round scores flow through [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/competition-score-fns.ts#saveCompetitionScoreFn]], which for `time-with-cap` workouts derives per-round cap status server-side from each round's encoded value against `workout.timeCap * 1000`, persists that status on `scoreRoundsTable`, preserves the summed total on `scoresTable.scoreValue` (instead of clamping to the cap), and threads `cappedRoundCount` into `computeSortKey` so the leaderboard tiebreaker honors "fewer capped rounds wins".
107

file.content:
Configures the algorithm used to rank athletes on the leaderboard.

Fetches events for head-to-head tiebreaker selection. Uses `ScoringSettingsForm` component. Settings include scoring algorithm, point distribution, and tiebreak rules.

## Results Entry

Organizers enter scores for each athlete per event, or review video submissions for online competitions.

For **in-person competitions**: `ResultsEntryForm` provides a per-event, per-division score entry grid. Organizers select an event and division, then enter scores for each athlete. Supports publishing/unpublishing division results. Multi-round scores flow through [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/competition-score-fns.ts#saveCompetitionScoreFn]], which for `time-with-cap` workouts derives per-round cap status server-side from each round's encoded value against `workout.timeCap * 1000`, persists that status on `scoreRoundsTable`, preserves the summed total on `scoresTable.scoreValue` (instead of clamping to the cap), and threads `cappedRoundCount` into `computeSortKey` so the leaderboard tiebreaker honors "fewer capped rounds wins".

lines: Organizers enter scores for each athlete per event, or review video submissions for online competitions.

-For **in-person competitions**: `ResultsEntryForm` provides a per-event, per-division score entry grid. Organizers select an event and division, then enter scores for each athlete. Supports publishing/unpublishing division results. Multi-round scores flow through [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/competition-score-fns.ts#saveCompetitionScoreFn]], which for `time-with-cap` workouts derives per-round cap status server-side from each round's encoded value against `workout.timeCap * 1000`, persists that status on `scoreRoundsTable`, preserves the summed total on `scoresTable.scoreValue` (instead of clamping to the cap), and threads `cappedRoundCount` into `computeSortKey` so the leaderboard tiebreaker honors "fewer capped rounds wins".

+For **in-person competitions**: `ResultsEntryForm` provides a per-event, per-division score entry grid. Organizers select an event and division, then enter scores for each athlete. Supports publishing/unpublishing division results. Multi-round scores flow through [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/competition-score-fns.ts#saveCompetitionScoreFn]], which for `time-with-cap` workouts derives per-round cap status server-side from each round's encoded value against `workout.timeCap * 1000`, persists that status on `scoreRoundsTable`, preserves the summed total on `scoresTable.scoreValue` (instead of clamping to the cap), and threads `cappedRoundCount` into `computeSortKey` so the leaderboard tiebreaker honors "fewer capped rounds wins". The grid's existing-score fetch (and every downstream write/delete) scopes by `data.divisionId` so an athlete registered in multiple divisions for a shared workout surfaces a separate score row per division — see [[lat.md/domain#Domain Model#Scoring#One score per athlete per event per division]].

For **online competitions**: Shows a submissions overview with links to individual video verification pages at `/events/{eventId}/submissions/`. Layout mirrors the volunteer review index (`/compete/$slug/review`) — parent events render as cards with their child sub-events listed inline, each row showing per-event total / reviewed / pending counts and a progress bar. Counts come from [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/video-submission-fns.ts#getSubmissionCountsByEventFn]], which gates on `requireSubmissionReviewAccess(competitionId)`, filters the requested trackWorkoutIds to those belonging to the competition's programming track (so callers can't enumerate counts across tenants), then issues grouped `COUNT() ... GROUP BY trackWorkoutId` queries for total and reviewed counts — autochunked as needed to respect MySQL parameter limits. It inner-joins `competition_registrations` to exclude submissions from removed registrations, matching the leaderboard and in-person results entry filter. This replaces the prior per-event `getEventSubmissionsFn` fan-out, which materialized full submission rows (with autochunked user/division/registration lookups) just to compute three numbers per event. The per-event review list ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/video-submission-fns.ts#getOrganizerSubmissionsFn]]) applies the same removed-registration filter on its registration join.

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(scoring): scope scores by division to prevent cross-leaderboard leaks

The scoresTable unique index was `(competition_event_id, user_id)`, so an
athlete registered in multiple divisions (e.g. individual + partner) sharing
the same workout could only hold ONE row — `onDuplicateKeyUpdate` collapsed
every submission into it, and every leaderboard/athlete-detail read joined
on (event, user) with no division filter, so whichever division was written
last appeared on every registration the user owned.

Widen the unique index to (competition_event_id, user_id, scaling_level_id),
then scope every score read, write, and delete by divisionId:
- write paths require/propagate divisionId and reject ambiguous multi-division
  submissions instead of silently picking a registration
- competition + series leaderboards bucket by score.scalingLevelId (ground
  truth) and match each score to its exact (user, division) registration
- organizer athlete detail, division results counts, and score deletes stay
  scoped per-division so shared-workout scores in other divisions aren't
  surfaced or nuked as collateral

Column-name gotcha: scores.competition_event_id actually stores a
track_workouts.id, not a competition_events.id — documented in the invariant
section and the SQL header so future readers aren't misled. The migration
itself is a simple index widen with no data risk (the old 2-col unique
guarantees at most one row per (event, user) pair).

Run apps/wodsmith-start/sql/score-division-unique.sql against the DB before
deploying. lat.md/domain.md documents the new invariant and the write/read/

input.command:
gh pr create --title "fix(scoring): scope scores by division to prevent cross-leaderboard leaks" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

- `scoresTable` unique index was `(competition_event_id, user_id)`, so an athlete registered in multiple divisions (e.g. Individual + Partner) sharing the same workout could only hold ONE row. `onDuplicateKeyUpdate` collapsed every submission into it, and every leaderboard/athlete-detail read joined on `(event, user)` with no division filter, so whichever division wrote last appeared on every registration the user owned.
- Widens the unique index to `(competition_event_id, user_id, scaling_level_id)` and scopes every score read/write/delete by `divisionId`. Write paths now reject ambiguous multi-division submissions instead of silently picking one. Leaderboards bucket by `score.scalingLevelId` (ground truth) and match each score to its exact `(user, division)` registration. Deletes stay scoped per-division so a different division's score on a shared workout isn't collateral damage.
- Column-name gotcha: `scores.competition_event_id` actually stores a `track_workouts.id`, not a `competition_events.id`. Documented in the invariant section and the SQL header.

## Deploy steps

1. Apply `apps/wodsmith-start/sql/score-division-unique.sql` against the DB before rolling out the code. The DDL is a simple index widen — safe to run against existing data because the old 2-col unique guarantees at most one row per `(event, user)` pair, so broadening to 3 cols cannot produce collisions.
2. Deploy the code.

## Test plan

- [ ] Register a test user for both an individual and a partner division in an online competition that shares a workout across both divisions.
- [ ] Submit a score for the partner division. Confirm it shows on the partner leaderboard preview and on the partner registration detail page.
- [ ] Confirm the individual division's leaderboard preview does NOT show that partner score, and the individual registration detail page does NOT show it either.
- [ ] Submit a separate score for the individual division. Confirm both scores persist and each is attributed to the correct registration.
- [ ] Delete the partner-division score via the organizer athlete detail card. Confirm the individual-division score is untouched.
- [ ] Organizer results entry grid: open the per-event grid filtered by each division, confirm only that division's scores are shown.
- [ ] Series leaderboard (if applicable): confirm scores in mapped divisions are attributed to the correct series division.
- [ ] Registration removal (single-division athlete): confirm unchanged behavior — scores in that competition are cleaned up.

## Documentation

- `lat.md/domain.md` — new `Scoring#One score per athlete per event per division` section describing the unique key and write/read/delete contracts, plus the `competition_event_id` column-name gotcha.
- `lat.md/organizer-dashboard.md` — cross-links from Results Entry, Leaderboard Preview, and Athlete Detail Page into the new invariant.
- `lat.md/registration.md` — cross-link from Multi-Division Registration.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -10
